books.title: Shub Down & Small-up Yuself!
books.subtitle: Diaries of Jamaica by Bus
books.author: Susan Lowe
books.description: Hilarious and thoughtful tales of bus rides in corporate Kingston and rural Jamaica with Jamaican glossary. Experience authentic Jamaican culture through vibrant conversations and memorable encounters.
books.longDescription: A vibrant journey through Jamaica's countryside by bus, capturing the essence of island life, colorful characters, and the unique patois that brings the narrative to life. This collection of diaries offers readers an insider's view of Jamaican culture, from the bustling streets of Kingston to the serene rural landscapes. Susan Lowe masterfully blends humor, insight, and cultural observation to create a truly authentic Caribbean experience. Hit #1 in Amazon's Caribbean UK & Germany stores.

books.title: Yu Get Jook!
books.subtitle: Diaries of a Jamaican Medic
books.author: Susan Lowe
books.description: An insider's view of healthcare in Jamaica, blending medical practice with cultural insights. Follow a dedicated medic's journey through the challenges and triumphs of providing care in the Caribbean.
books.longDescription: A Jamaican doctor's intimate journey into and through medicine, offering readers a unique perspective on healthcare in the Caribbean. Susan Lowe draws on her extensive experience as both a medical doctor and therapist to present authentic stories of patient care, medical challenges, and the human connections that make medicine meaningful. From emergency rooms to community clinics, these diaries reveal the heart of Jamaican healthcare with humor, compassion, and unflinching honesty.

books.title: The Brown Phoenix
books.subtitle: An Erotic Diary from the Indies
books.author: Susan Lowe (as M.T. Lowe)
books.description: A sensual and provocative exploration of love, desire, and self-discovery in the Caribbean. A mature, literary work that pushes boundaries while celebrating Caribbean sensuality.
books.longDescription: An intimate and unapologetically erotic diary that explores themes of passion, identity, and liberation in the West Indies. Through lyrical prose and candid reflection, this work celebrates Caribbean sensuality while examining the complexities of desire and human connection. Written with literary sophistication and cultural authenticity, The Brown Phoenix offers mature readers a thought-provoking journey through love, loss, and rebirth in a tropical paradise. This is a bold exploration of the intimate self, not for the faint of heart.

books.title: Wings After Fairborough
books.author: Susan Lowe
books.description: A story of resilience, transformation, and finding freedom after trauma. Follow the journey of healing and rediscovery as the protagonist spreads their wings toward a new life.
books.longDescription: Carl Hostrup, a twice-failed-in-marriage, 60-year-old retired business executive and brilliant USAF bomber captain from WWII, illegally flies into restricted missile base airspace in a vintage aircraft after a USAF reunion in fictitious Fairborough, UK. Upon arrest, he reconnects with Sally, his wartime lover, and discovers an unknown teenage orphan granddaughter named Sheila. This powerful narrative explores themes of redemption, family secrets, and second chances, written in the curl-up-with-a-good-book style of the 1980s. A touching exploration of love, legacy, and the courage to start anew.

books.title: On the Great Barrier
books.author: Susan Lowe
books.description: An adventure exploring the wonders and fragility of the Great Barrier Reef ecosystem. Environmental consciousness meets gripping narrative in this tale of marine exploration.
books.longDescription: Dive into an unforgettable adventure exploring one of Earth's most magnificent natural wonders—the Great Barrier Reef. This compelling narrative weaves together environmental awareness, marine biology, and human drama as characters navigate the delicate balance between preservation and progress. Through vivid descriptions and scientifically-informed storytelling, Susan Lowe brings readers face-to-face with the beauty and vulnerability of our ocean ecosystems. A timely and gripping tale that will change how you see our planet's precious coral reefs.

author.name: Susan Lowe
author.bio: Susan Lowe is a multi-faceted author, integrative general practitioner, transformational coach, and licensed therapist whose diverse experiences inform her rich storytelling. With a background in healthcare spanning over 29 years and counseling, she brings authenticity and depth to her narratives exploring Caribbean life, medical practice, human relationships, and environmental themes. Born in Fresno, California in 1958, Susan is a first-generation American who has made Jamaica her home, immersing herself in the vibrant culture that inspires much of her work.
